explanation: The phrase "Once..." suggests an eventual occurrence, which maps to the 'F' (finally) operator. The condition "the power of the system has been off for more than 15 minutes" is a specific duration. In LTL, a duration of 'N' time steps for a proposition 'P' is typically represented as 'P & X P & X X P & ... & X^N P'. Assuming each time step represents one minute, "more than 15 minutes" means at least 16 consecutive minutes of being 'off'. Thus, the condition becomes 'off & X off & X X off & X X X off & X X X X off & X X X X X off & X X X X X X off & X X X X X X X off & X X X X X X X X off & X X X X X X X X X off & X X X X X X X X X X off & X X X X X X X X X X X off & X X X X X X X X X X X X off & X X X X X X X X X X X X X off & X X X X X X X X X X X X X X off & X X X X X X X X X X X X X X X off'. Let's call this long conjunction 'OffLongEnough'. The consequence "it shall not be possible to select “Resume Ventilation”" means that 'resumeVentilation' must be false, so '!resumeVentilation'. The phrase "shall not be possible" (from that point onwards) implies a global constraint from the moment the condition is met, thus 'G(!resumeVentilation)'. Combining these, the structure "Once A, then B forever" translates to F(A -> G B). Therefore, the full formula is F(OffLongEnough -> G(!resumeVentilation)).
